config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, mudah sekali terjadi penyalahgunaan oleh negara atau mayoritas terhadap kelompok kecil. Saya paham bahwa dalam banyak situasi kepentingan bersama penting, misalnya soal kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi itu tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar orang per orang. Dari sudut pandang saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi martabat dan kebebasan individu terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach by government or a loud majority. Public health and safety matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and minority protections, or else “the common good” becomes a blank check. I also do not think individual rights are just selfish preferences; they are what keep society legitimate in the first place. If society needs power, it should earn that power by showing necessity, narrow limits, and real accountability.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara mutlak atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pemaksaan, sementara yang paling rentan justru menanggung akibatnya. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that usually becomes a justification for overreach by government or the majority. The phrase “for the common good” can sound noble, but without strict limits it can erase due process, minority rights, and basic freedoms that are supposed to be nonnegotiable. Society absolutely has legitimate needs, especially in emergencies, but those claims should be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable rather than treated as automatically superior. In my view, a society is only worth trusting when it can protect the public without treating individual rights as optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih tinggi daripada hak individu secara umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, dan justru di situlah bahaya utamanya: hak minoritas bisa hilang hanya karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi ketat oleh hak dasar, karena masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah memaksa, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi martabat setiap orang. Dalam keadaan darurat mungkin ada pembatasan, tetapi itu harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak dijadikan alasan permanen untuk mengorbankan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify government overreach and majority rule without enough restraint. Public safety and the common good matter, but they are not a free pass to weaken due process, free speech, or minority protections whenever it is politically convenient. I also think the strongest societies are not the ones that demand the most sacrifice from individuals, but the ones that can meet public needs while still treating rights as real limits, not optional privileges. In emergencies, limited restrictions can be justified, but only if they are necessary, proportional, and tightly accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
